content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum


To : Jeanne                                    Date : 6-15-76

                                              [illegible]

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Dawn

Subject: Correspondence with Richard Swan

At 3:10 p.m. on this date I telephoned Richard Swan,
Judge of the Vallejo-Benicia Municipal Court. As Mr. Swan
was not in I spoke to Mrs. Lois Gibson, his secretary,
and informed her that Mr. Swan's Statement of Economic
Interests will be due within 30 days after assuming his
new term of office, which will be in January, 1977.
She understood.

I can see this appears to be a mostly blank document with only a few small marks visible at the top of the page (what appear to be two curved marks or parentheses and some small marks on the right side). The rest of the page is white/blank.

Since the document contains no legible text to transcribe, here is what I can report:

[Page contains minimal visible content - only faint curved marks visible near top of page]

Vallejo-Benicia Municipal Court
County of Solano
Vallejo, California 94590

Chambers of [illegible]
RICHARD J. SWAN
Judge

Vallejo Justice Building
321 TUOLUMNE STREET
(707) 553-5271

June 14, 1976

Technical Assistance & Analysis Div.
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Gentlemen:

The purpose of this letter is to determine whether
I am to file a Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721)
in July of 1976 or January 1977.

In July 1975, I was in my fifth year of my third
6-year term as Judge of the Benicia Justice Court. Judge
Wallace Cox was in his fifth year of his term of office
as Judge of the Vallejo Municipal Court. On July 3, 1975,
Judge Cox retired and the Solano County Board of Supervisors
merged the Vallejo Municipal Court and the Benicia Justice
Court, said merger to coincide with the day Judge Cox retired.
I therefore, pursuant to Government Code Section 71083(a),
automatically took Judge Cox's position on the newly created
Vallejo-Benicia Municipal Court.

On June 8 of this year, I was re-elected without
opposition.

Please advise me of the proper month in which I must
file the aforementioned Form 721.

Very truly yours,

RICHARD J. SWAN, JUDGE
Vallejo-Benicia Municipal Court

RJS/lg

[The image appears to be a very faded or low-contrast document scan. Most of the text is illegible or extremely difficult to read with confidence. The document appears to have some text at the top and scattered text throughout the page, but the content is too faint to transcribe accurately without significant guessing. There appear to be some markings or stamps on the page as well.]

I apologize, but I cannot reliably transcribe this document due to the extremely poor contrast and legibility of the text. To provide you with an accurate transcription following your rules, I would need a clearer, higher-contrast version of this image.

As Mr. Swan was not in I spoke to Mrs. Lois Gibson, his secretary, and informed her that Mr. Swan's Statement of Economic Interests will be due within 30 days after assuming his new term of office, which will be in January, 1977. She understood. The image you provided is completely white, with no visible content or text. If you have another image or a different question, please provide it for better results. If you have another page or a higher quality scan, please provide it for better results.
